doctrine: Suspected invariants we don't have data sources for yet. Each one is a possible blind spot in the discovery engine. REALITY-CHECKED each sweep against live data — a factor that IS being fetched/computed will not be listed here, so this list can't go stale and send you chasing data you already have.

unknown_factors.name: long_short_ratio
unknown_factors.description: Aggregate Binance + Bybit long/short ratio. Hypothesis: extreme long bias is the #1 retail FOMO signal at tops.

unknown_factors.name: cvd_btc
unknown_factors.description: Cumulative Volume Delta on BTC. Buyers vs sellers in actual control.

unknown_factors.name: exchange_reserves
unknown_factors.description: BTC exchange reserves. Distribution = top signal, accumulation = bottom signal.

unknown_factors.name: options_skew
unknown_factors.description: BTC options put/call skew. Institutional hedging behavior. NOTE: no free US-legal source — Deribit (primary skew venue) is US-banned, Glassnode options is Professional-tier only. Known blind spot until a paid US-legal source is added.
unknown_factors.data_source_needed: Paid US-legal options data (Amberdata/Laevitas) — none free

unknown_factors.name: kalshi_poly_implied_probs
unknown_factors.description: Implied probabilities for major macro events from Kalshi + Polymarket. Crowd wisdom.
unknown_factors.data_source_needed: kalshi_bot already has Kalshi API access; just needs to expose the data

_note: 2 previously-listed gap(s) are now LIVE and filtered out: realized_vol_7d_30d, copper_gold_ratio
